elements.title: Quincy Market
elements.contents: ... that Boston's Quincy Market (building pictured), once described by the city's mayor as an "eyesore", later became more popular than Disney World?

elements.title: Winifred Lewellin James
elements.contents: ... that after divorcing her husband, Winifred Lewellin James was required to report to a police station each time she went more than 5 miles (8 km) from her home?

elements.title: fuel-tax evasion scheme
elements.contents: ... that a fuel-tax evasion scheme in Mexico involves importing fuels under unrelated customs categories?

elements.title: Steve Potter
elements.contents: ... that Steve Potter fired his father?

elements.title: National Cold Fusion Institute
elements.contents: ... that an independent panel reviewing research conducted by the National Cold Fusion Institute found no evidence that cold fusion had occurred?

elements.title: Salem Methodist Church
elements.contents: ... that the Salem Methodist Church in Harlem operated a boxing club in its basement?

elements.title: the current mayor of Nagoya
elements.contents: ... that the current mayor of Nagoya once operated a computer shogi program that won a world championship?

elements.title: Melania
elements.contents: ... that two-thirds of the New York production staff for Melania requested to be omitted from the credits?

elements.title: Rosa Praed
elements.contents: ... that the novelist Rosa Praed believed that she and her medium were the reincarnations of an ancient Roman slave girl and slave mistress?
